title: betula utilis subsp. occidentalis
intro: Betula utilis subsp. occidentalis, also known as Himalayan white birch, is a subspecies of birch tree native to the western Himalayas. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can help to soothe skin irritations and infections. The health benefits of Himalayan white birch include reducing inflammation, improving skin health, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Himalayan white birch can be prepared as a tea, salve, or infusion, or used in herbal remedies such as ointments and creams to harness its medicinal properties.
